rationale: Both candidates independently catch the two stale YAML doc comments (prefix-caching rationale, max_num_batched_tokens header), but X also surfaces the seed-reproducibility tradeoff under full cudagraphs that was the crux of gcanlin's inline thread — Y misses it entirely. Neither flags the core 'cudagraph_mode shouldn't live in the model layer' design critique that dominated the GT discussion. Y's OOM claim leans on a suspiciously specific unverifiable doc citation (docs/serving/speech_api.md:667) and its 'missing test' findings are generic absence-of-evidence asks rather than grounded defects, dragging down precision; X's line-level tracing through the runner/wrapper path is more consistent and its asks (rewrite comment, drop redundant line, add A/B) are more concrete.
